signatures.label: calc(expression)
signatures.documentation: Performs mathematical calculations for CSS property values. Supports +, -, *, / operators. Can mix different units (px, %, rem, vw, vh, em). Spaces required around + and - operators.

parameters.label: expression
parameters.documentation: A mathematical expression using +, -, *, / operators. Can mix units: calc(100% - 2rem), calc(1rem + 0.5vw), calc(var(--x) * 2).

signatures.label: var(--custom-property, fallback)
signatures.documentation: Substitutes the value of a CSS custom property. Custom properties must start with --. Falls back to the second argument if the property is undefined or invalid.

parameters.label: --custom-property
parameters.documentation: The name of the CSS custom property to reference. Must include the -- prefix (e.g., --primary-color, --spacing-unit).

parameters.label: fallback
parameters.documentation: Optional fallback value used when the custom property is not defined. Can itself contain var() references.

signatures.label: min(value1, value2, ...)
signatures.documentation: Returns the smallest of the comma-separated values. Useful for responsive sizing: min(100%, 600px) ensures an element never exceeds 600px.

parameters.label: value1
parameters.documentation: First value to compare. Can be any CSS length, percentage, or calc() expression.

parameters.label: value2
parameters.documentation: Second value (and more) to compare against. Accepts the same types as value1.

signatures.label: max(value1, value2, ...)
signatures.documentation: Returns the largest of the comma-separated values. Useful for minimum sizing: max(300px, 50%) ensures at least 300px wide.

parameters.label: value1
parameters.documentation: First value to compare.

parameters.label: value2
parameters.documentation: Second value (and more) to compare against.

signatures.label: clamp(min, preferred, max)
signatures.documentation: Clamps a value between a minimum and maximum bound. Equivalent to max(MIN, min(VAL, MAX)). Ideal for fluid typography and responsive sizing.

parameters.label: min
parameters.documentation: The minimum allowed value. The result will never be smaller than this.

parameters.label: preferred
parameters.documentation: The preferred/ideal value that will be used if it falls between min and max. Often uses viewport units (vw).

parameters.label: max
parameters.documentation: The maximum allowed value. The result will never be larger than this.

signatures.label: rgb(red, green, blue)
signatures.documentation: Specifies a color using the RGB color model. Each channel accepts an integer (0–255) or percentage (0%–100%). Modern syntax uses spaces instead of commas with optional alpha: rgb(R G B / alpha).

parameters.label: red
parameters.documentation: Red channel: 0–255 or 0%–100%.

parameters.label: green
parameters.documentation: Green channel: 0–255 or 0%–100%.

parameters.label: blue
parameters.documentation: Blue channel: 0–255 or 0%–100%.

signatures.label: hsl(hue, saturation, lightness)
signatures.documentation: Specifies a color using the HSL color model. More intuitive than RGB for color manipulation. Modern syntax uses spaces: hsl(H S L / alpha).

parameters.label: hue
parameters.documentation: Hue angle: 0–360 (0/360=red, 120=green, 240=blue). Accepts deg, rad, grad, turn units.

parameters.label: saturation
parameters.documentation: Saturation: 0% (gray) to 100% (full color).

parameters.label: lightness
parameters.documentation: Lightness: 0% (black) to 50% (pure color) to 100% (white).

signatures.label: oklch(lightness, chroma, hue)
signatures.documentation: Specifies a color in the OKLCH perceptually uniform color space (CSS Color Level 4). Better for generating consistent palettes than HSL.

parameters.label: lightness
parameters.documentation: Perceptual lightness: 0 (black) to 1 (white), or 0%–100%.

parameters.label: chroma
parameters.documentation: Chroma (colorfulness): 0 (gray) to ~0.4 (most vivid). Not bounded like saturation.

parameters.label: hue
parameters.documentation: Hue angle: 0–360. Similar to HSL hue but perceptually uniform.

signatures.label: linear-gradient(direction, color-stop1, color-stop2, ...)
signatures.documentation: Creates a linear gradient image transitioning between two or more colors along a straight line.

parameters.label: direction
parameters.documentation: Gradient direction: angle (e.g., 45deg, 135deg) or keyword (to top, to right, to bottom left). Default: to bottom (180deg).

parameters.label: color-stop1
parameters.documentation: First color and optional position: color [percentage/length]. E.g., #ff0000 0%, red 50px.

parameters.label: color-stop2
parameters.documentation: Subsequent color stops. Multiple stops create multi-color gradients.

signatures.label: radial-gradient(shape size at position, color-stop1, color-stop2, ...)
signatures.documentation: Creates a radial (circular/elliptical) gradient image radiating from an origin point.

parameters.label: shape
parameters.documentation: Shape: circle or ellipse (default). Affects how the gradient radiates.

parameters.label: size
parameters.documentation: Size keyword: closest-side, closest-corner, farthest-side, farthest-corner (default). Or explicit radius.

parameters.label: position
parameters.documentation: Center position: default is center center. Accepts lengths, percentages, or keywords (top, left, etc.).

parameters.label: color-stops
parameters.documentation: Two or more color stops with optional position percentages.

signatures.label: url(path)
signatures.documentation: References an external resource by URL. Used for images, fonts, stylesheets, cursors, and other assets.

parameters.label: path
parameters.documentation: URL string — can be absolute, relative, or data URI. Quotes are optional but recommended for URLs with special characters.

signatures.label: env(variable, fallback)
signatures.documentation: Accesses environment variables defined by the user agent. Commonly used for safe area insets on mobile devices.

parameters.label: variable
parameters.documentation: Environment variable name: safe-area-inset-top, safe-area-inset-right, safe-area-inset-bottom, safe-area-inset-left, titlebar-area-x, titlebar-area-y, titlebar-area-width, titlebar-area-height.

parameters.label: fallback
parameters.documentation: Optional fallback value if the environment variable is not available.

signatures.label: translate(x, y)
signatures.documentation: CSS transform function that repositions an element along the X and Y axes. Does not affect document flow.

parameters.label: x
parameters.documentation: Horizontal offset: length or percentage. Positive moves right.

parameters.label: y
parameters.documentation: Optional vertical offset: length or percentage. Positive moves down. If omitted, defaults to 0.

signatures.label: rotate(angle)
signatures.documentation: CSS transform function that rotates an element around its transform-origin point.

parameters.label: angle
parameters.documentation: Rotation angle. Accepts deg, rad, grad, turn units. Positive = clockwise, negative = counter-clockwise.

signatures.label: scale(x, y)
signatures.documentation: CSS transform function that resizes an element along the X and Y axes.

parameters.label: x
parameters.documentation: Horizontal scale factor: 1 = original size, 2 = double, 0.5 = half.

parameters.label: y
parameters.documentation: Optional vertical scale factor. If omitted, defaults to the same value as x (uniform scaling).

signatures.label: attr(attribute-name)
signatures.documentation: Returns the value of an HTML attribute of the selected element. Currently only works reliably in the content property of pseudo-elements in most browsers.

parameters.label: attribute-name
parameters.documentation: The name of the HTML attribute to retrieve (e.g., data-label, href, title).
